note: Cycle 47 merge — 17 existing patterns updated from 25 new signals. 5 patterns marked declining (no signal in 29+ cycles). 1 new pattern added: agent-architecture-strategy-discussion (first seen cycle 47).

patterns.label: Recurring Meeting Agenda Generation & Prep
patterns.llmRationale: Confirmed this cycle via Standing Team/V-Team Weekly Sync (freq=3, 150min), 1:1 and Small-Group Design Reviews (freq=3, 90min), and Full-Day Workshop (freq=1, 480min). Meeting structures remain highly templatable; agenda generation from title + attendee set continues to be a prime automation target.

patterns.label: Azure DevOps Notification Triage
patterns.llmRationale: ADO Notification Triage confirmed via email (freq=25, 60min) and Teams cross-source ADO flow (freq=8, 30min). Cross-source signal now adds Teams-side deduplication and ownership discussion as a second stage — strengthens the DevOps-Passive-Intake named pipeline. Classification remains fully rule-based.

patterns.label: Eval Design Coaching & SME Consultation
patterns.llmRationale: Confirmed via Eval Tooling Technical Q&A (teams, freq=10, 90min), Customer/Partner Eval Deep Dive (meeting, freq=2, 120min), and cross-source Customer Ask to Eval Deep Dive chain (freq=2, 90min). PM remains sole DRI. Highest value score in catalog at 92. EvalCon deadline April 2 amplifying urgency.

patterns.label: Meeting Notes & Action Item Capture (Loop)
patterns.llmRationale: Confirmed via cross-source Meeting to Teams Follow-ups to Document Updates (freq=3, 45min). Camp AIR sessions generating follow-up document chains is a stable recurring instance of this pattern. Loop-to-structured-notes automation remains high-confidence.

patterns.label: JSON Config & Agent State File Authoring
patterns.llmRationale: Strong document signal this cycle: Skill Framework & Automation Artifact Authoring (freq=7, 180min), Agent Runtime & Dev Artifact Management (freq=5, 60min), Cycle Dashboard Creation (freq=3, 90min), and Dev Environment Setup (freq=2, 30min). Combined document authoring time of 6hrs in structured automation artifacts — schema-driven generation remains a core automation opportunity.

patterns.label: Post-Meeting External Follow-Up Email Drafting
patterns.llmRationale: File-Share Review Notifications (freq=4, 15min) provide a lighter-touch confirmation of external document-sharing follow-up patterns. Customer/Partner Eval Deep Dive meetings generate structured follow-up needs. Pattern remains stable but low volume this cycle.

patterns.label: Training Event & Conference Artifact Coordination
patterns.llmRationale: Strong multi-source confirmation this cycle: Full-Day Workshop / Camp AIR (meeting, 480min), EvalCon Coordination email nudges (freq=3), EvalCon Planning Teams thread (freq=5), Camp AIR Logistics & Artifact Sharing (teams, freq=6), plus two cross-source event chains. Combined 20 new occurrences and 11.8 hrs. EvalCon April 2 deadline amplifying trend — RISING.

patterns.label: Technical Artifact & Repo Sharing in Teams
patterns.llmRationale: Confirmed via Collaborative Docs Shared via Teams (Loop & Decks, doc/teams, freq=6, 45min) and Camp AIR Logistics & Artifact Sharing (teams, freq=6, 45min). Camp AIR immersion cascade is amplifying artifact sharing volume — apply 0.7x trend dampening for cascade attribution. Underlying pattern is still genuinely rising.

patterns.label: VoC Executive Summary & MPR Blurb Drafting
patterns.llmRationale: ADO Triage & Customer-Ask Deduplication (teams, freq=8, 60min) maps to this pattern — deduplication decisions are a form of VoC routing and exec-summary generation. Pattern is expanding beyond pure MPR blurbs into deduplication outputs. Automation opportunity growing.

patterns.label: Daily Planning Page & Day-Prep Artifact Creation
patterns.llmRationale: No signal detected in cycle 47 (last seen cycle 18, 29 cycles ago). Dev Environment Setup & Shell Scripting signal is adjacent but not a match. Marked declining — may be seasonal or crowded out by higher-priority document authoring. Monitor for resurrection.

patterns.label: Parallel Eval & Discovery Deck Proliferation
patterns.llmRationale: Partial confirmation via Collaborative Docs / EvalCon planning artifacts — parallel deck creation for Camp AIR and EvalCon by multiple participants. Cascade-inflated this cycle (Camp AIR immersion). Apply 0.7x dampening. Underlying pattern stable.

patterns.label: Company-Wide Digest & Broadcast Email Triage
patterns.llmRationale: No signal detected in cycle 47 (last seen cycle 18, 29 cycles ago). EvalCon Coordination signal is directional email but is event-specific, not broadcast digest. Marked declining — high automation score but absent from recent activity.

patterns.label: AI Skills Library Design & Workflow Standardization
patterns.llmRationale: Skill Framework & Automation Artifact Authoring (doc, freq=7, 180min) is a direct confirmation — skill-detector, Skilluminator, SKILL.md, and dashboard.html creation are active workflow standardization work. Rising due to increased Skilluminator cycle cadence.

patterns.label: In-Thread PM Status Update Contributions
patterns.llmRationale: No direct signal detected in cycle 47 (last seen cycle 18, 29 cycles ago). EvalCon coordination nudges are event-specific rather than recurring status updates. Marked declining.

patterns.label: Compliance & Security Alert Classification
patterns.llmRationale: Strong cycle 47 confirmation: Security & DLP Compliance Remediation (email, freq=6, 45min) and cross-source Security Alert to File Edit to Compliance Resolution (freq=4, 20min). The 3-step deterministic loop (Purview alert -> OneDrive file edit -> unblock confirmation) is confirmed as a named pipeline. Auto score raised to 87 — this is the highest-confidence compliance automation in the catalog. Rising trend, now a named pipeline (Security-Alert-to-Remediation).

patterns.label: PRD / Spec / SDR Format Fragmentation
patterns.llmRationale: No signal detected in cycle 47 (last seen cycle 18, 29 cycles ago). Document activity this cycle is focused on agent/skill artifacts rather than PRD/SDR formats. Marked declining.

patterns.label: Short Acknowledgement & Courtesy Reply Writing
patterns.llmRationale: No signal detected in cycle 47 (last seen cycle 18, 29 cycles ago). Email activity this cycle dominated by system notifications and event coordination, not courtesy replies. Marked declining.

patterns.label: Agent Architecture & Cross-Org Strategy Discussion
patterns.llmRationale: New pattern first seen cycle 47: Agent Architecture & Cross-Org Strategy Discussion (teams, freq=4, 75min) spanning PM, Architect, Engineering Lead, and Leadership. Topics: A365 cross-org observability, non-M365 agents, vision and strategy. Strategic content resists full automation (auto=52), but Claude can synthesize discussion threads into structured decision memos, open-question trackers, and architecture decision records (ADRs). High value score (76) due to 4-role participation and downstream impact on org-wide architecture decisions.

candidateSkills.name: ado-notification-router
candidateSkills.description: Classifies and routes Azure DevOps work-item notification emails (Tasks, Bugs, Requirements, Epics, Features) by type, project, priority, and ownership. Extends into the Teams triage step of the DevOps-Passive-Intake pipeline.
triggerExamples: I have 20+ unread ADO notification emails — help me triage them
New ADO work items keep coming in for evals and IcM — route these to the right owners
My ADO email folder is full — classify and summarize what needs action today
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Highest single-source frequency in catalog (63+ occurrences). Classification is 100% rule-based by work-item type, project prefix, and keyword — zero human judgment required per item. Eliminates 1.5+ hrs/week of manual triage.

candidateSkills.name: eval-design-advisor
candidateSkills.description: Answers repeated eval methodology questions from engineers, field roles, and customers: test-case counts, pass rate thresholds, eval-driven development sequencing, grader selection, CLI usage, and production readiness criteria.
triggerExamples: How many test cases do I need for a statistically significant eval?
What pass rate threshold should I use for my eval suite?
Walk me through eval-driven development — where do I start?
I'm getting CSV import errors in the eval CLI — what do I check?
candidateSkills.valueProposition: PM is sole DRI for 36+ repetitive eval guidance requests per cycle. Highest value score in catalog (92). Unblocks BSI 89 CRITICAL bottleneck. EvalCon deadline April 2 amplifying urgency — builds a durable knowledge asset beyond the event.

candidateSkills.name: event-artifact-coordinator
candidateSkills.description: Generates pre-event prep packs, session agendas, participant briefings, artifact trackers, and post-event summaries for multi-day workshops and conferences (Camp AIR, EvalCon, v-team offsites).
triggerExamples: Camp AIR is next week — create a prep pack for our breakout session
EvalCon is April 2 — draft the agenda and participant guidance
We just finished Camp AIR — generate the post-event summary and artifact index
candidateSkills.valueProposition: 20 new occurrences and 11.8 hrs in a single cycle. Rising trend with two active events (Camp AIR, EvalCon). Cross-source coordination across email, Teams, meetings, and documents — structured outputs reduce 4+ hours of manual coordination per event.

candidateSkills.name: compliance-remediation-assistant
candidateSkills.description: Handles the 3-step Purview/DLP compliance remediation loop: receive security alert, classify severity and identify the flagged file, draft remediation action (sanitize credentials, re-save, or dispute), and confirm resolution.
triggerExamples: I got another Purview 'File Blocked' alert — what do I do?
DLP flagged a file I modified in OneDrive — help me resolve it
I have 6 security alerts from this week — triage and resolve them
candidateSkills.valueProposition: 13 occurrences this cycle — most deterministic 3-step compliance loop in the catalog (auto score 87, ceiling 91-94%). Confirmed as a named pipeline (Security-Alert-to-Remediation). Zero human judgment calls in the standard path. Eliminates 1+ hr/week of repetitive compliance remediation.

candidateSkills.name: meeting-notes-action-extractor
candidateSkills.description: Converts meeting transcripts or chat context into structured Loop-format notes: Agenda, Notes, Decisions, Open Questions, Action Items with owners and due dates, Next Meeting.
triggerExamples: The Camp AIR session just ended — extract action items and decisions
Here's the transcript from our v-team sync — create the Loop notes
I need to write up the design review with my manager — draft it from my notes
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Chain head pattern with 9+ occurrences and 91 automation score. SGI 92 (CRITICAL standardization gap in meeting notes structure). Upstream of transcript-to-loop and weekly-status report patterns.

candidateSkills.name: architecture-discussion-summarizer
candidateSkills.description: Synthesizes agent architecture and cross-org strategy discussions into structured decision memos, open-question trackers, and architecture decision records (ADRs) from Teams chat threads.
triggerExamples: We had a big A365 architecture thread — summarize the key decisions and open questions
Capture the cross-org agent strategy discussion as an ADR
Extract the architecture decisions from this Teams thread for the leadership deck
candidateSkills.valueProposition: New pattern this cycle — 4 participants including Leadership, 75min of discussion. Strategic synthesis skill that converts high-value but unstructured cross-org conversations into durable decision artifacts. High value (76) despite moderate automation (52).
